_comment: Performance index for Blueprint layout decisions. Updated by @review after each build where outcome signals are available. Blueprint reads this in Step 5 before committing to a layout pattern. Signal values: high / medium / low. Confidence: high (N≥10), medium (N≥3), low (N<3). Do not fabricate signals — only record observations from real deployments or strong qualitative evidence.

observations.notes: Kakurega wholesale inquiry page — qualitative only. Journal-sequential created appropriate friction for a professional B2B context; no casual conversion pressure. Fit matched the 'every word matters' tone from Q7.

journal-sequential.notes: Full-width image / narrow text rhythm reads as editorial confidence. Wrong for any context where speed of comprehension matters more than quality of attention.

editorial-alternating.notes: The alternating rhythm implies multiple distinct things to show. Wrong when the brand has one strong product or one strong idea — journal-sequential handles that better.

cinematic.notes: Full-bleed 80-100vh sections generate attention; wrong when the audience needs to think rather than feel.

data-dense.notes: Density signals that the builder trusts the reader. Wrong when warmth or discovery is part of the product promise.

credential.notes: One focal element per viewport says 'I am enough.' Wrong when the product needs to explain itself across multiple feature dimensions.

document.notes: Reads as written, not designed — intentional for technical audiences who distrust marketing.

gallery.notes: Image grid as primary content works when the product IS the visual. Wrong when the visual illustrates a service or idea.
